mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the unemployment rate signals rapid labor market tightening, which historically precedes positive equity returns as wage growth supports consumer demand and corporate profits.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in >50% of instances where UNRATE drops >0.2pp in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives earnings revisions upward and lifts the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon by confirming sustainable economic growth.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises >1.0% month-over-month during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive short-term equity returns as investors price in higher near-term cash flows.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit zero or negative correlation with quarterly GDP growth acceleration over any 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation spike exceeding 0.5% triggers market expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market boom, which historically sustains positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1000 thousand units signal robust construction activity and consumer confidence, which drives positive short-term equity returns as financials and materials sectors outperform.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months where HOUST remains above 1000 thousand units for three consecutive months.
